metadata.description: Three datasets for micro/macro analysis of public-sector workers, institutional independence, and social protection in developing democratizers. (1) WVS Wave 7 individual-level survey (576 examples): employment sector × institutional trust × SocProt/Gini quadrant — the primary dataset for hypothesis testing. (2) V-Dem democracy indicators (1102 examples): country-year democracy scores and judicial corruption for developing democratizer classification and macro context. (3) ILO SocProt country-year (354 examples): social protection coverage for quadrant classification validation.
source_datasets: WVS Wave 7 (Gabors subset 2000): individual survey, employment sector + institutional trust
ILO SDG 1.3.1 via DBnomics: country-year social protection coverage
V-Dem v16: country-year democracy + institutional quality indicators

examples.input: Country: ARG | Year: 2017 | Sector: ngo | Education: 1 | Income quintile: 1 | Social protection coverage: 67.0% | Gini: 37.9 | Quadrant: high_socprot_low_gini
examples.output: Trust judiciary: 1 | Trust government: 1 | Trust parties: 1 | Democracy preference: None | Democracy importance: None | Institutional independence index: 0.3333

examples.input: Country: ARG | Year: 2017 | Sector: private | Education: 1 | Income quintile: 1 | Social protection coverage: 67.0% | Gini: 37.9 | Quadrant: high_socprot_low_gini
examples.output: Trust judiciary: 2 | Trust government: 2 | Trust parties: 1 | Democracy preference: None | Democracy importance: None | Institutional independence index: 0.5556

examples.input: Country: ARG | Year: 2017 | Sector: public | Education: 1 | Income quintile: 2 | Social protection coverage: 67.0% | Gini: 37.9 | Quadrant: high_socprot_low_gini
examples.output: Trust judiciary: 3 | Trust government: 3 | Trust parties: 3 | Democracy preference: None | Democracy importance: None | Institutional independence index: 1.0
Corruption judiciary: -2.034 | Corruption executive: 0.932 | Civil liberties: 0.542
